publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-02-21 18:37:46 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The collection includes reports, correspondence, documents, official publications, photographs and statements pertaining to Burma and the American Karen Agency from Klein's time as the Director of Human Rights for the AKA.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Harold Eugene Klein was born in 1926 in Greeley, Colorado. His parents were missionaries to Burma, where he grew up until moving back to the USA in 1940. He attended Denison University, before transferring to University of Redlands, and once more to UC Berkeley, where he graduated with a BA in Political Science. He also obtained his Teacher's Credential with studies at UCLA, as well as a Masters of Arts from Long Beach State University and a began (but never completed) his Ph.D. at UCLA. Klein worked as a high school teacher until his retirement in 1985. Following his retirement, he began working with the American Karen Agency as the Director of Human Rights, raising funds and goods for refugee camps, hospitals, and schools in Burma.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The collection includes reports, correspondence, documents, official publications, photographs and statements pertaining to Burma and the American Karen Agency from Klein's time as the Director of Human Rights for the AKA. Notable among the papers are letters to and from politicians, including senators, representatives, and the President. Also includes material from The Overseas Burma Liberation Organization, Karen Human Rights Group, and Amnesty International.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Harold E. Klein papers, #6796. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Thailand to Harold Klein

scopecontent.p: Letters from Harold Klein to people Letters from people to Harold Klein List about Keren Names and addresses in USA Articles Information about family tree of Rev. Saw Tun Sein Appendix about delta self support rural rehabilitation and human development project Refugee inquiry sheet (near East and South Asia) from Church World Service Ohn Han's Curriculum Vitae Copies of certification from United Nations Application for resettlement in the States Appendix about Karen rural development Chris`tn center project An Eyewitness Account of the Damages and Loss of Lives Suffered by Villagers in the Delta Area During Fighting with the SLORC Army. 1991. scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: International Narcotics Control Strategy Report March 1988 United States Mission to the United Nations: The U.S. Government is aware of human rights problems in Burma. U.S. policy towards Burma. Bureau of Refugee Program: the policy of Thai government which has the right to arrest Burmese students on the frontier and deport them. Burma American Fund is being awarded approximately $49,000 for distribution to Burmese refugees along the border. Outline of Testimony to be presented before the congressional Human Rights Caucus Hearings on Burma Letters from American Karen Agency to the United States Department of State U.S. Relations with East Asia Harold Klein's concerns on U.S. foreign policy toward Burma and its minority groups Report on the trip to Thailand and Burma from Feb. 9 to 23, 1990 by Sao Ying Sita of Yawnghwe Letter to President George Bush on Sept. 28, 1990 Letter from Philip R. Mayhew, Director, Office of Thailand and Burma Affairs, stating that The U.S. government encouraged the Royal Thai Government to act against narcotics trafficking in the region but not including sealing the border against the Karen people. Perspectives on Economic and Environmental Problems of Burma. The Fight for Freedom and Democracy in Burma by Paula Dobriansky. Crisis in Burma : Background Burma Election Statement by the President to ask Burma's military rulers to release Aung San Suu Kyi
